title: petasites frigidus subsp. articus
intro: Petasites frigidus subsp. articus, also known as the Arctic butterbur or Bear's garlic, is a plant species belonging to the family Compositae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of migraines and other conditions. The plant's extracts and oils have been used to create herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and topical creams, to promote health benefits such as reducing pain, inflammation, and improving sleep quality. However, it is essential to note that the plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which can be toxic in large quantities, and thus, proper dosing and preparation under medical supervision are crucial.
